events.title: NorCal Resist: Monthly Free Auto Light Check and Repair
events.description: On the first Saturday of every month, a group of local volunteers will test and replace: brake lights, head lights, turn signals, and running lights for free. 10:00 am – 2:00 pm at the Sierra Gold Community Senior Center in Grass Valley. We provide all the lights!
events.venue_address: Sierra Gold Community Senior Center, 231 Colfax Avenue, Grass Valley, CA, 95945, United States

events.title: "The Winters Where Catfish Sings": Exhibition by Sokthea Chan at Axis Gallery
events.description: The Winters Where Catfish Sings is a solo exhibition by Sokthea Chan that introduces a body of work inspired by the natural world, mythological stories, and poetry – from Friday, March 6th through Sunday, March 29th. The second Saturday reception will be from 5:00 – 8:00 pm on March 14th. The title of the exhibition draws its inspiration from cultural folklore that gives slight humor and sorrow to let the viewers to wonder and question its silly, bizarre name in relation to the work. events.venue_address: Axis Gallery, 625 S Street, Sacramento, CA, 95811, United States

This session is perfect for both new and experienced fosters who want to feel more confident and supported. events.venue_address: BriarPatch Co-op Grass Valley: Community Room, 290 Sierra College Drive, Grass Valley, CA, 95945, United States

events.title: Las Ninas Muertas: a Happy Hour Show at the Crazy Horse
events.description: Tucked away in the foothills of California, a band of renegade women honor the music of the Grateful Dead. Las Ninas Muertas, a five-piece, mostly acoustic band plays your favorite Dead tunes. Imbued with the spirit of love for this timeless songbook, Las Ninas Muertas sing in four-part harmony, with searing harmonica and electric guitar riffs, the twang of banjo, and the twinkle of mandolin all sitting on the foundation of upright bass. Las Ninas Muertas invite you to dance, sing along, and com
events.venue_address: Crazy Horse Saloon, 230 Commercial Street, Nevada City, CA, 95959, United States

events.title: Nevada County Skate: Second Annual Spaghetti Feed Fundraiser
events.description: Please join us for a night of good food, good company, and a great cause from 6:00 – 9:00 pm on Saturday, March 7th at the Grass Valley Elks Lodge! events.venue_address: Grass Valley Elks Lodge, 109 South School Street, Grass Valley, CA, 95945, United States

events.venue_address: Crazy Horse Saloon, 230 Commercial Street, Nevada City, CA, 95959, United States

events.title: Queer Sangha at Mountain Stream Meditation
events.description: Queer Sangha is a peer-led meditation and community group for all who find affinity in queerness – every second and fourth Sunday from 6:30 – 8:00 pm. This is an in-person event only, as we come together to practice meditation and build community, resilience, and solidarity through Sangha – a Buddhist word for the community who practices together. Our gatherings include: a 30-minute meditation (seated, lying down, or standing poses are all very welcome), a time to share and listen to each other,
events.venue_address: Mountain Steam Meditation, 710 Zion Street, Nevada City, CA, 95959, United States

events.title: Nevada City Monthly Death Cafe
events.description: Death . . . what are your feelings around death? Welcome to the Nevada City Death Cafe, where we invite you to join us in a relaxed, quiet setting to discuss and share thought-provoking, engaging, and respectful conversations about death. There is no set agenda, and ALL are invited to share their feelings and experiences with death, and what is most important to us in our finite lives. Tea and cake will be served. The Nevada County local Death Cafe meets the second Monday of every month from 4:3
events.venue_address: Madelyn Helling Library, 980 Helling Way, Nevada City, CA, 95959, United States

events.description: Please join us for the Yin Yoga Series at Sierra College Nevada County Campus, Community Education which meets from 5:30 – 6:30 pm on Monday evenings, March 2nd through 30th, 2026 (there will be no class on March 23rd) . Yin is a restorative style of yoga that works deep into the connective tissue for stretching and lengthening. events.venue_address: Sierra College Community Education, 250 Sierra College Dr., Grass Valley, CA, 95945, United States

events.title: An Evening with Michael Pollan at the Crest
events.description: Please join KVIE for an enlightening evening with acclaimed author Michael Pollan, in conversation with Donna Apidone, as he discusses his highly anticipated new book, A World Appears: A Journey into Consciousness from 7:00 – 8:00 pm on Monday, March 9th at the Crest Theatre in Sacramento. In this captivating event, he will discuss the mysteries of the mind, the nature of awareness, and what it means to perceive the world around us. Each admission ticket includes a signed copy of A World Appears
events.venue_address: Crest Theater, 1013 K St, Sacramento, CA, United States
